translations.en: I’d like to reserve a table.
translations.nl: Ik wil graag een tafel reserveren.
translations.hi: मैं एक मेज़ आरक्षित करना चाहता हूँ.
translations.el: Θα ήθελα να κλείσω ένα τραπέζι.
translations.sv: Jag skulle vilja boka ett bord.
translations.ro: Aș dori să rezerv o masă.
translations.ja: テーブルを予約したいです。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun, nominative singular, 1st person; clausal subject controlling agreement of the finite auxiliary.
Modal auxiliary (1st person singular, present Konjunktiv II of ‘mögen’) in verb-second position; expresses polite desire and selects the bare infinitive ‘reservieren’.
Indefinite article, masculine accusative singular; determiner of the noun ‘Tisch’.
Common noun, masculine accusative singular; direct object (theme) of ‘reservieren’.
Main verb in bare infinitive; complements the modal ‘möchte’ and occurs clause-finally; denotes the act of booking.

sentences.target: Im Zug wollen wir Plätze reservieren.

translations.de: Im Zug wollen wir Plätze reservieren.
translations.en: We want to reserve seats on the train.
translations.es: Queremos reservar asientos en el tren.
translations.fr: Nous voulons réserver des places dans le train.
translations.it: Vogliamo prenotare dei posti sul treno.
translations.ru: Мы хотим забронировать места в поезде.
translations.vi: Chúng tôi muốn đặt chỗ trên tàu.
translations.nl: We willen plaatsen reserveren in de trein.
translations.uk: Ми хочемо забронювати місця в поїзді.
translations.hi: हम ट्रेन में सीटें आरक्षित करना चाहते हैं.
translations.el: Θέλουμε να κλείσουμε θέσεις στο τρένο.
translations.nb: Vi vil reservere plasser på toget.
translations.id: Kami ingin memesan kursi di kereta.
translations.sv: Vi vill boka platser på tåget.
translations.ro: Vrem să rezervăm locuri în tren.
translations.cs: Chceme si zarezervovat místa ve vlaku.
translations.da: Vi vil reservere pladser i toget.
translations.ja: 列車の座席を予約したいです。

tokens.explanation: Fused preposition+article (‘in dem’); heads a locative prepositional phrase and (as a fused form) governs dative ‘Zug’.
Common noun, masculine dative singular; object of the preposition ‘im’, setting the location context.
Modal auxiliary (1st person plural, present) in verb-second position after the fronted PP; agrees with ‘wir’ and selects the infinitive ‘reservieren’.
Personal pronoun, nominative plural, 1st person; clausal subject of ‘wollen’.
Common noun, plural accusative; bare plural direct object of ‘reservieren’.
Main verb in bare infinitive; complements the modal ‘wollen’ and denotes the booking action.

sentences.target: Meine Eltern möchten ein Zimmer reservieren.

translations.de: Meine Eltern möchten ein Zimmer reservieren.
translations.en: My parents would like to reserve a room.
translations.es: Mis padres quieren reservar una habitación.
translations.fr: Mes parents voudraient réserver une chambre.
translations.it: I miei genitori vorrebbero prenotare una camera.
translations.zh-Hans: 我父母想预订一个房间。
translations.pt: Meus pais querem reservar um quarto.
translations.tr: Annemle babam bir oda ayırtmak istiyor.
translations.vi: Bố mẹ tôi muốn đặt phòng.
translations.nl: Mijn ouders willen een kamer reserveren.
translations.hi: मेरे माता-पिता एक कमरा आरक्षित करना चाहते हैं.
translations.el: Οι γονείς μου θέλουν να κλείσουν ένα δωμάτιο.
translations.nb: Foreldrene mine vil bestille et rom.
translations.id: Orang tua saya ingin memesan kamar.
translations.sv: Mina föräldrar vill boka ett rum.
translations.ro: Părinții mei vor să rezerve o cameră.
translations.cs: Moji rodiče si chtějí zarezervovat pokoj.
translations.da: Mine forældre vil gerne bestille et værelse.
translations.ja: 両親は部屋を予約したいです。

tokens.explanation: Possessive determiner; agrees with the plural nominative head ‘Eltern’ and marks possession.
Plural-only common noun, nominative plural; clausal subject of ‘möchten’.
Modal auxiliary (3rd person plural, present Konjunktiv II of ‘mögen’) expressing polite desire; selects the infinitive ‘reservieren’.
Indefinite article, neuter accusative singular (form identical to nominative); determiner of ‘Zimmer’.
Common noun, neuter accusative singular; direct object of ‘reservieren’.
Main verb in bare infinitive; complements the modal ‘möchten’ and denotes the booking action.

sentences.target: Tom muss den Raum für morgen reservieren.
sentences.reading: /tɔm mʊs deːn ʁaʊ̯m fyːɐ̯ ˈmɔʁɡn̩ ʁezɛʁˈviːʁən/

translations.de: Tom muss den Raum für morgen reservieren.
translations.ipa: /tɔm mʊs deːn ʁaʊ̯m fyːɐ̯ ˈmɔʁɡn̩ ʁezɛʁˈviːʁən/
translations.en: Tom has to reserve the room for tomorrow.
translations.es: Tom tiene que reservar la sala para mañana.
translations.fr: Tom doit réserver la salle pour demain.
translations.it: Tom deve prenotare la sala per domani.
translations.ko: 톰은 내일 사용할 회의실을 예약해야 해요.
translations.zh-Hans: 汤姆得为明天预订会议室。
translations.yue: 湯姆要為聽日預約會議室。
translations.ru: Тому нужно забронировать комнату на завтра.
translations.pt: Tom precisa reservar a sala para amanhã.
translations.he: טום צריך להזמין את החדר למחר.
translations.tr: Tom yarın için odayı ayırtmak zorunda.
translations.vi: Tom phải đặt phòng cho ngày mai.
translations.ar: يجب على توم حجز الغرفة ليوم غد.
translations.nl: Tom moet de ruimte voor morgen reserveren.
translations.uk: Том має забронювати кімнату на завтра.
translations.hu: Tomnak holnapra le kell foglalnia a termet.
translations.hi: टॉम को कल के लिए कमरा आरक्षित करना है.
translations.pl: Tom musi zarezerwować pokój na jutro.
translations.el: Ο Τομ πρέπει να κλείσει την αίθουσα για αύριο.
translations.nb: Tom må reservere rommet til i morgen.
translations.id: Tom harus memesan ruangan untuk besok.
translations.sv: Tom måste boka rummet till i morgon.
translations.ro: Tom trebuie să rezerve sala pentru mâine.
translations.cs: Tom musí zarezervovat místnost na zítra.
translations.da: Tom skal reservere lokalet til i morgen.
translations.ja: トムは明日のために部屋を予約しないといけません。

tokens.explanation: Proper name, nominative singular; clausal subject of the finite auxiliary.
Modal auxiliary (3rd person singular, present) expressing obligation; finite verb selecting the infinitive ‘reservieren’.
Definite article, masculine accusative singular; determiner of ‘Raum’.
Common noun, masculine accusative singular; direct object of ‘reservieren’.
Preposition introducing a temporal adjunct (‘für morgen’); typically governs accusative with noun phrases; here combines with the temporal adverb.
Temporal adverb specifying the intended time; complements the preposition ‘für’ within a PP.
Main verb in bare infinitive; complements the modal ‘muss’ and denotes the booking action.
